verdict.summary: Round 3 resolves both round-2 major findings. The security-models record now qualifies the honest-majority UC statement with the required authenticated/private communication resources, broadcast where required, and UC session/scheduling assumptions, while explicitly describing applicability to the current in-process implementation as aspirational. The capability matrix now limits the secure_equal result to a masked-opening distribution lemma, carries the bit-leaking F_join into any future composition proof, and leaves realization conditional on a distributed protocol and full-view simulator. The new posture record consistently preserves those limitations, describes the semi-honest and malicious-opener boundaries, keeps collaborative proving fail-closed, and retains the sq-qhy4 external-audit caveat. No correctness, soundness, test-validity, security, or instruction-injection issue remains in the diff.
